title: Terminalia selloana
taxonomy: Terminalia selloana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae. It belongs to the genus Terminalia, which includes over 200 species of trees and shrubs. The Combretaceae family comprises about 650 species of plants, most of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is native to Central and South America. Terminalia selloana is a small tree or shrub that grows in a variety of habitats.

intro: Terminalia selloana, also known as Sello's terminalia or palo verde, is a deciduous tree native to South America, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive green bark. The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Terminalia selloana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Terminalia selloana is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, allowing for its bioactive constituents to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
